calendar.matters:
Inheritance Funding Company Incs Petition For Court Determination As To Whether Probate Advances Assignment Of Beneficiary Donte Henrys Rebate Is Valid Pursuant To Probate Code Section 11604.5
Off Calendar Oct-08-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven D. Schroeder, Esq., counsel for Inheritance Funding Co. Inc., Assignee (appearing in person); Constance Figuers, Esq., counsel for Probate Advance LC, Assignee (appearing by video conference - audio only).

Counsel report the various circumstances giving rise to today's proceedings: whether the Probate Advance/Donte Henry agreement of a rebate/remaining interest in the estate to Probate Advance constitutes an assignment within the scope of Probate Code Sec. 11604.5 and if so, whether the assignment complies with the requirements of that Code section.

The Court advises counsel that it tentatively finds the Probate Advance agreement is an assignment under Probate Code Sec. 11604.5 which Ms. Figuers contests. Court and counsel discuss whether the assignment then meets the requirements of Probate Code Sec. 11604.5.

After discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission with the stated intent to find that the Probate Advance agreement is an assignment under Probate Code Sec. 11604.5, does comply with the requirements of that Code section, and that any rebate/remaining interest funds held by Inheritance Funding should be paid to Probate Advance. Upon inquiry by the Court, Mr. Schroeder advises the Court that a proposed Order was submitted yesterday [October 6, 2020].

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Bifurcated Issue Of Probate Advance'S Entitlement To Donte Henry'S Rebate
Off Calendar Oct-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven D. Schroeder, Esq., counsel for Inheritance Funding Co. Inc., Assignee (appearing in person); Constance Figuers, Esq., counsel for Probate Advance LC, Assignee (appearing by video conference - audio only).

Counsel report the various circumstances giving rise to today's proceedings: whether the Probate Advance/Donte Henry agreement of a rebate/remaining interest in the estate to Probate Advance constitutes an assignment within the scope of Probate Code Sec. 11604.5 and if so, whether the assignment complies with the requirements of that Code section.

The Court advises counsel that it tentatively finds the Probate Advance agreement is an assignment under Probate Code Sec. 11604.5 which Ms. Figuers contests. Court and counsel discuss whether the assignment then meets the requirements of Probate Code Sec. 11604.5.

After discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission with the stated intent to find that the Probate Advance agreement is an assignment under Probate Code Sec. 11604.5, does comply with the requirements of that Code section, and that any rebate/remaining interest funds held by Inheritance Funding should be paid to Probate Advance. Upon inquiry by the Court, Mr. Schroeder advises the Court that a proposed Order was submitted yesterday [October 6, 2020].

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (D204)

Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Advanced From Apr-29-20 To Apr-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Bifurcated Issue Of Probate Advance'S Entitlement To Mr. Henry'S Rebate
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jul-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Inheritance Funding Company Incs Petition For Court Determination As To Whether Probate Advances Assignment Of Beneficiary Donte Henrys Rebate Is Valid Pursuant To Probate Code Section 11604.5
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jul-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-19-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Shustoff and Nora Woodring for and with Richard Billups.
Constance Figuers for Probate Advance.
Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to issue of whether or not the petition for final distribution should be resolved today, the dispute being between the funding companies and it having no affect on final distribution of the estate, it not being fair to the Beneficiaries to have to wait until April for their distribution, Donte Henry's assignment of the rebate to probate advance holding up the estate, whether or not the issue of said assignment is subject to California Law or New York Law, whether Probate Code Section 11604.5 applies for the Court's review to determine if the amount is reasonable, etc. Discussion was also held as to an appropriate form of order, the amount of the rebate not being reduced, the issue of Mr. Henry's rebate being bifurcated and heard with Inheritance Funding's petition, etc. The Court noted that no one was present from American Research. All Counsel indicated that they have no issue with the amount that is going to American Research. The Court granted the Petition for Final Distribution, etc. (subject to receiving and reviewing a revised order) and orders all distributions as requested except that any issue concerning Probate Advance's entitlement to Mr. Henry's rebate is bifurcated and is to be decided on April 28, 2020. All Parties present in Court today are to sign/approve the proposed order as to form. The Parties are to submit any further argument no later than April 21, 2020. Inheritance Funding Company Inc.'s Petition for Court Determination as to Whether Probate Advance's Assignment of Beneficiary Donte Henry's Rebate is Valid Pursuant to Probate Code Section 11604.5 is advanced from April 29, 2020 to April 28, 2020.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
